_description: Standard NLP benchmark scores for the Base Llama 3.1 8B model (meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B, FP16 on the dissertation test system). Primary source: Meta AI Llama 3.1 technical report (Dubey et al., 2024). Additional evaluations run locally using the lm-evaluation-harness framework on the RX 9060 XT system.

_sources.primary: Dubey, A. et al. (2024). The Llama 3 Herd of Models. Meta AI. arXiv:2407.21783.

MMLU.description: 57-subject multiple-choice exam covering STEM, humanities, social sciences, etc. Tests breadth of world knowledge.

MMLU_Pro.description: More challenging version of MMLU with 10-option questions, requiring deeper reasoning.

ARC_Challenge.description: Science exam questions requiring multi-step reasoning, not answerable by simple information retrieval.
ARC_Challenge.source: Open LLM Leaderboard / lm-eval-harness local run

TruthfulQA.description: Tests whether a model generates truthful answers to adversarially chosen questions designed to elicit common misconceptions.
TruthfulQA.notes: Base (non-instruction-tuned) models typically score lower than chat-tuned variants.

WinoGrande.description: Pronoun resolution adversarially collected to test commonsense reasoning.

HellaSwag.description: Sentence completion benchmark testing commonsense NLI / grounded language understanding.

BoolQ.description: Reading comprehension task requiring yes/no answers to naturally occurring questions.

GSM8K.description: 8,500 grade-school maths word problems requiring multi-step arithmetic reasoning.
GSM8K.notes: Base model (no RLHF). Instruct model would score significantly higher.

HumanEval.description: 164 hand-written Python programming problems; tests functional code generation.
HumanEval.notes: Base model only. Instruction-tuned variant achieves ≈ 72.6%.

MATH.description: Competition mathematics problems across 7 subjects (algebra, geometry, number theory, etc.).
MATH.notes: Base model; challenging benchmark. Instruct variant: ≈ 51.9%.

WikiText2_Perplexity.description: Perplexity on the WikiText-2 test set. Lower is better. Measures the model's language-modelling ability.
WikiText2_Perplexity.source: Local measurement using lm-eval-harness sliding window (stride 512)
WikiText2_Perplexity.notes: Measured on the local dissertation system (RX 9060 XT). Results align with published Llama 3.1 8B perplexity.

comparative_context.note: Approximate scores for comparable open-source base models at time of writing.

key_findings: Llama 3.1 8B achieves 66.7% on MMLU, a significant improvement over its predecessor Llama 2 7B (45.3%) and competitive with larger 13B models from the previous generation.
Strong commonsense reasoning (HellaSwag 82.1%, WinoGrande 78.6%) confirms broad language understanding at this parameter scale.
Mathematical reasoning (GSM8K 56.7%) shows the base model can perform multi-step arithmetic, though instruction-tuned variants are substantially stronger.
Code generation (HumanEval 33.5%) is functional but limited without fine-tuning — a clear direction for future improvement.
TruthfulQA (44.7%) reveals the base model sometimes perpetuates common misconceptions; RLHF fine-tuning substantially improves this.
WikiText-2 perplexity of 6.24 (FP16) is consistent with published figures, validating the local deployment configuration.
